paper.title: Gaia EDR3 in 6D: Searching for unbound stars in the Galaxy

review.summary: Paper releases a FITS catalogue of distances and total velocities for ~7 million Gaia EDR3 stars with DR2 radial velocities. Contains 6 LaTeX tables: 2 method tables (quality cuts, potential parameters), 2 sample catalogs (17 and 12 high-velocity candidates), 1 derived orbit table, and 1 catalogue schema table. External resource: FITS catalogue download at author's website.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Documents the quality cuts applied to select a clean subset of high velocity stars from the full Gaia EDR3+DR2 sample, showing the number of stars surviving each cut.

source_refs.caption: Summary of the cuts employed to select a clean subset of high velocity stars. The column $N_\mathrm{stars}$ denotes the number of stars in {\Gaia} EDR3 surviving each cut. The bulk of the analysis of this paper relies on the sample of 6969738 stars with precise astrometry from {\Gaia} EDR3 and radial velocities from {\Gaia} DR2.
source_refs.label: tab:cuts

columns.name: Cut
columns.meaning: Description of the quality cut applied

columns.name: N_stars
columns.meaning: Number of stars in Gaia EDR3 surviving the cut

internal_tables.evidence: Summary of the cuts employed to select a clean subset of high velocity stars. The column $N_\mathrm{stars}$ denotes the number of stars in {\Gaia} EDR3 surviving each cut. The bulk of the analysis of this paper relies on the sample of 6969738 stars with precise astrometry from {\Gaia} EDR3 and radial velocities from {\Gaia} DR2.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the characteristic mass and scale parameters of the gala MilkyWayPotential components (bulge, nucleus, disk, halo) used for orbit integration.

source_refs.caption: Characteristic scale parameters used in the \textsc{gala} potential \textsc{MilkyWayPotential} \citep{bovy15, gala}.
source_refs.label: tab:pot_params

columns.name: Component
columns.meaning: Galactic component (Bulge, Nucleus, Disk, Halo) of the MilkyWayPotential

columns.name: Parameters
columns.meaning: Mass and scale radius/height parameters for each Galactic component

internal_tables.evidence: Characteristic scale parameters used in the \textsc{gala} potential \textsc{MilkyWayPotential} \citep{bovy15, gala}.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents observed and derived properties for the clean sample of 17 high velocity stars with Pub > 0.8 of being unbound from the Milky Way. This is the primary result table of the paper.

source_refs.caption: Observed and derived properties for the clean sample of 17 high velocity stars with a probability $P_\mathrm{ub} > 0.8$ of being unbound from the Milky Way. Quantities derived from the orbital integration are reported in Table \ref{tab:candidates_orb}. Stars are ordered by decreasing values of $P_\mathrm{ub}$.
source_refs.label: tab:candidates

columns.name: Gaia EDR3 ID
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 source identifier

columns.name: (RA, Dec.)
columns.meaning: Right ascension and declination (ICRS) of the star

columns.name: (l, b)
columns.meaning: Galactic longitude and latitude of the star

columns.name: varpi
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 parallax with uncertainty

columns.name: mu_alpha*
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 proper motion in right ascension (cos(delta) corrected) with uncertainty

columns.name: mu_delta
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 proper motion in declination with uncertainty

columns.name: v_rad
columns.meaning: Gaia DR2 radial velocity with uncertainty

columns.name: G
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 G-band mean magnitude

columns.name: d
columns.meaning: Median distance estimate derived by inverting MC parallax samples, with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties

columns.name: r_GC
columns.meaning: Median spherical Galactocentric radius, with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties

columns.name: v_GC
columns.meaning: Median total velocity in the Galactocentric rest-frame, with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties

columns.name: P_MW
columns.meaning: Probability that the star originated from the Milky Way disk (fraction of MC realizations with r_min < 25 kpc)

columns.name: P_ub
columns.meaning: Probability that the star is unbound from the Milky Way (fraction of MC realizations with v_GC > escape speed at the star's position)

internal_tables.evidence: Observed and derived properties for the clean sample of 17 high velocity stars with a probability $P_\mathrm{ub} > 0.8$ of being unbound from the Milky Way. Quantities derived from the orbital integration are reported in Table \ref{tab:candidates_orb}. Stars are ordered by decreasing values of $P_\mathrm{ub}$.
internal_tables.comments: Stars are split into 11 Galactic (P_MW > 0.5) and 6 extragalactic (P_MW < 0.5) stars.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents orbital parameters (Galactocentric cylindrical coordinates, energy, minimum crossing radius, ejection velocity, flight time) derived from backward orbit integration for the 17 candidates with Pub > 0.8.

source_refs.caption: Galactocentric cylindrical coordinates $(R_\mathrm{GC}, z_\mathrm{GC})$, energy per unit mass $E$, minimum crossing radius $r_\mathrm{min}$, ejection velocity $v_\mathrm{ej}$, and flight time $t_\mathrm{F}$ for the clean sample of 17 stars with $P_\mathrm{ub} > 0.8$. Stars are ordered as in Table \ref{tab:candidates}.
source_refs.label: tab:candidates_orb

columns.name: Gaia EDR3 ID
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 source identifier

columns.name: R_GC
columns.meaning: Median Galactocentric cylindrical radius, with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties

columns.name: z_GC
columns.meaning: Median Galactocentric height above the disk plane, with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties

columns.name: E
columns.meaning: Median orbital energy per unit mass, with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties; E > 0 indicates unbound orbit

columns.name: r_min
columns.meaning: Median minimum crossing radius (distance from Galactic Centre at disk crossing), with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties; defined only for Galactic stars

columns.name: v_ej
columns.meaning: Median ejection velocity (total velocity at disk crossing), with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties; defined only for Galactic stars

columns.name: t_F
columns.meaning: Median flight time (time between disk crossing and current observation), with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties; defined only for Galactic stars

internal_tables.evidence: Galactocentric cylindrical coordinates $(R_\mathrm{GC}, z_\mathrm{GC})$, energy per unit mass $E$, minimum crossing radius $r_\mathrm{min}$, ejection velocity $v_\mathrm{ej}$, and flight time $t_\mathrm{F}$ for the clean sample of 17 stars with $P_\mathrm{ub} > 0.8$. Stars are ordered as in Table \ref{tab:candidates}.
internal_tables.comments: Extragalactic stars have no r_min, v_ej, or t_F values since they never cross the Galactic disk.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents observed and derived properties for 12 high velocity stars with Pub > 0.5 when including the Gaia EDR3 parallax zero point correction, serving as a comparison to the main candidate table.

source_refs.caption: Observed and derived properties for the clean sample of 12 high velocity stars with a probability $P_\mathrm{ub} > 0.5$ of being unbound from the Milky Way, computed including the estimate of the parallax zero point $\varpi_\mathrm{zp}$. Stars are ordered by decreasing values of $P_\mathrm{ub}$.
source_refs.label: tab:candidates_zp

columns.name: Gaia EDR3 ID
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 source identifier

columns.name: (RA, Dec.)
columns.meaning: Right ascension and declination (ICRS) of the star

columns.name: (l, b)
columns.meaning: Galactic longitude and latitude of the star

columns.name: varpi - varpi_zp
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 parallax minus the estimated parallax zero point, with uncertainty

columns.name: mu_alpha*
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 proper motion in right ascension (cos(delta) corrected) with uncertainty

columns.name: mu_delta
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 proper motion in declination with uncertainty

columns.name: v_rad
columns.meaning: Gaia DR2 radial velocity with uncertainty

columns.name: G
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 G-band mean magnitude

columns.name: d
columns.meaning: Median distance estimate (after zero point correction), with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties

columns.name: r_GC
columns.meaning: Median spherical Galactocentric radius, with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties

columns.name: v_GC
columns.meaning: Median total velocity in the Galactocentric rest-frame, with 16th and 84th percentile uncertainties

columns.name: P_MW
columns.meaning: Probability that the star originated from the Milky Way disk

columns.name: P_ub
columns.meaning: Probability that the star is unbound from the Milky Way

internal_tables.evidence: Observed and derived properties for the clean sample of 12 high velocity stars with a probability $P_\mathrm{ub} > 0.5$ of being unbound from the Milky Way, computed including the estimate of the parallax zero point $\varpi_\mathrm{zp}$. Stars are ordered by decreasing values of $P_\mathrm{ub}$.
internal_tables.comments: 7 Galactic and 5 extragalactic stars. Only 3 have Pub > 0.8. 5 stars are in common with Table 3 (tab:candidates).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Documents the 60 columns of the released FITS catalogue containing derived distances and velocities for ~7 million Gaia EDR3 stars with DR2 radial velocities.

source_refs.caption: Description of the columns of the catalogue with distances and velocities. Entries labelled $^1$ are taken directly from the {\Gaia} EDR3 catalogue, while entries labelled $^2$ are derived in this paper. We refer the interested reader to Section \ref{sec:method} for details on how these quantities are computed. For the entries labelled $^2$, the quoted values correspond to the median of the distribution, with lower and upper uncertainties computed, respectively, from the $16$th and the $84$th percentile. Column 60 is present only in the version of the catalogue which includes the effect of the parallax zero point.
source_refs.label: tab:catalogue

columns.name: Column
columns.meaning: Column number in the FITS catalogue (1-60)

columns.name: Units
columns.meaning: Physical units of the column value

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Column name as given in the FITS file header

columns.name: Description
columns.meaning: Description of the column content, with superscript 1 for Gaia-sourced values and 2 for values derived in this paper

internal_tables.evidence: Description of the columns of the catalogue with distances and velocities. Entries labelled $^1$ are taken directly from the {\Gaia} EDR3 catalogue, while entries labelled $^2$ are derived in this paper. We refer the interested reader to Section \ref{sec:method} for details on how these quantities are computed. For the entries labelled $^2$, the quoted values correspond to the median of the distribution, with lower and upper uncertainties computed, respectively, from the $16$th and the $84$th percentile. Column 60 is present only in the version of the catalogue which includes the effect of the parallax zero point.
internal_tables.comments: Columns 1-19 are Gaia-sourced; columns 20-59 are derived (distances, positions, velocities, Pub); column 60 (parallax_zp) only in zero-point-corrected version.

external_resources.description: Author's website hosting the FITS catalogue with derived distances and total velocities for ~7 million Gaia EDR3 stars with DR2 radial velocities. Two versions available: standard catalogue and version including parallax zero point correction.

source_refs.context: This work has made use of data from the European Space Agency (ESA) mission {\Gaia}, publicly available at the {\Gaia} archive. The catalogues derived in this work can be downloaded \href{https://sites.google.com/view/tmarchetti/research}{here}.

external_resources.evidence: Data Availability section: 'The catalogues derived in this work can be downloaded here: https://sites.google.com/view/tmarchetti/research'. Also referenced in abstract footnote and Section 3.
external_resources.comments: This URL appears multiple times in the paper (abstract footnote, Section 3, Data Availability section, Appendix B). The catalogue is a FITS file.
